title: Mughlai Murgh Malai Kebab (Tawa)

ingredients.name: boneless chicken breast or thigh, cut into large pieces

subs.name: large paneer cubes — cook 2–3 minutes per side; fragile so handle gently
extra cream — omitting curd makes the marinade richer and slightly less tangy
2 tbsp thick cream cheese or malai — same richness

steps.text: Whisk together cream, hung curd, grated cheese, ginger-garlic paste, green chilli, cardamom powder, black pepper, garam masala, lemon juice, and salt into a smooth white marinade. Coat the chicken pieces thoroughly, cover, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes — up to 4 hours for deeper flavour.

Bring the chicken to room temperature 15 minutes before cooking. Heat the tawa on high until very hot, then reduce to medium-high. Brush with oil. Lay the chicken pieces in a single layer without crowding.

Cook without moving for 4–5 minutes until the underside shows golden-brown char spots and the marinade has set. Flip carefully and cook for another 4–5 minutes. The chicken should register 75°C inside or cut through cleanly with no pink juice.
Baste with a little butter in the last minute for extra richness. Rest the kebabs on a warm plate for 2 minutes before serving with mint chutney, sliced onion rings, and lemon wedges.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cheese → 2 tbsp thick cream cheese or malai — same richness; chicken → large paneer cubes — cook 2–3 minutes per side; fragile so handle gently; curd → extra cream — omitting curd makes the marinade richer and slightly less tangy
